          simp [X₁]
        have h3 : ∫ ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X' i ω‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ.prod μ = ∫ ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, (X₁ i ω - X₂ i ω)‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ.prod μ := by
          simp [X', X₁, X₂]
        have h4 : ∫ ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω, ω)‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ ≤ ∫ ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, (X₁ i ω - X₂ i ω)‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ.prod μ := by
          -- Use the fact that the integral of the p-th power of the norm is less than or equal to the product measure
          have h5 : ∫ ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω, ω)‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ = ∫ ω : Ω, ∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω', ω')‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ ∂μ := by
            simp [MeasureTheory.integral_prod]
          have h6 : ∫ ω : Ω × Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, (X₁ i ω - X₂ i ω)‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ.prod μ = ∫ ω : Ω, ∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, (X₁ i (ω, ω') - X₂ i (ω, ω'))‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ ∂μ := by
            simp [MeasureTheory.integral_prod]
          have h7 : ∫ ω : Ω, ∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω', ω')‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ ∂μ ≤ ∫ ω : Ω, ∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, (X₁ i (ω, ω') - X₂ i (ω, ω'))‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ ∂μ := by
            -- Use the fact that the integral of the p-th power of the norm is less than or equal to the product measure
            calc
              ∫ ω : Ω, ∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω', ω')‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ ∂μ = ∫ ω : Ω, (∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω', ω')‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ) ∂μ := by
                simp [MeasureTheory.integral_prod]
              _ ≤ ∫ ω : Ω, (∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, (X₁ i (ω, ω') - X₂ i (ω, ω'))‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ) ∂μ := by
                -- Use the fact that the integral of the p-th power of the norm is less than or equal to the product measure
                refine' integral_mono (fun ω => _) (by positivity)
                have h8 : (∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω', ω')‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ) ≤ (∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, (X₁ i (ω, ω') - X₂ i (ω, ω'))‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ) := by
                  -- Use the fact that the integral of the p-th power of the norm is less than or equal to the product measure
                  have h9 : ∀ (ω : Ω), (∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω', ω')‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ) = (∫ ω' : Ω, ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω', ω')‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ) := by simp

                      -- Use the fact that the integral of the p-th power of the norm is less than or equal to the product measure

                linarith

          calc

        -- Use the fact that the integral of the norm power of the sum of X_i is bounded by the integral of the norm power of the sum of X'_i
        -- because the variables are zero mean and independent.
        calc
          ∫ (ω : Ω), ‖∑ i ∈ A, X i ω‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ
            = ∫ (ω : Ω), ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω, ω)‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ := by
              -- The term ∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω, ω) is equal to ∑ i ∈ A, X i ω
              congr with ω
              simp [X₁]
              <;>
              simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
              <;>
              rfl
            _ ≤ ∫ (ω : Ω), ‖∑ i ∈ A, X' i (ω, ω)‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ := by
              -- Use the inequality involving the norms and the fact that the variables are zero mean.
              have h₂ : ∀ (ω : Ω), ‖∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω, ω)‖ ≤ ‖∑ i ∈ A, X' i (ω, ω)‖ := by
                intro ω
                -- Prove the inequality for each ω.
                have h₃ : ∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω, ω) = ∑ i ∈ A, (X' i (ω, ω) + X₂ i (ω, ω)) := by
                  simp [X₁, X₂, X']
                  <;>
                  congr <;>
                  simp_all [Function.comp_apply, Finset.sum_add_distrib]
                  <;>
                  rfl
                have h₄ : ∑ i ∈ A, (X' i (ω, ω) + X₂ i (ω, ω)) = ∑ i ∈ A, X' i (ω, ω) + ∑ i ∈ A, X₂ i (ω, ω) := by
                  rw [Finset.sum_add_distrib]
                have h₅ : ∑ i ∈ A, X₂ i (ω, ω) = ∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω, ω) := by
                  simp [X₁, X₂]
                  <;>
                  congr <;>
                  simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
                  <;>
                  rfl
                have h₆ : ∑ i ∈ A, X' i (ω, ω) = ∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω, ω) - ∑ i ∈ A, X₂ i (ω, ω) := by
                  simp [X₁, X₂, X']
                  <;>
                  calc
                    ∑ i ∈ A, (X₁ i (ω, ω) - X₂ i (ω, ω)) = ∑ i ∈ A, (X₁ i (ω, ω) - X₂ i (ω, ω)) := rfl
                    _ = ∑ i ∈ A, X₁ i (ω, ω) - ∑ i ∈ A, X₂ i (ω, ω) := by
                      rw [Finset.sum_sub_distrib]
                calc
